name: perceived_security_trajectory
description: Measures respondents' assessments of how personal and national public security has changed over the past 12 months and how they expect it to change in the next 12 months, capturing both retrospective and prospective security evaluations at two levels of aggregation.

name: police_institutional_legitimacy
description: Measures the degree to which respondents view police as trustworthy, legitimate, and deserving of compliance, including performance ratings, satisfaction, perceived corruption, human rights violations, and normative acceptance of police authority.

name: crime_victimization_exposure
description: Measures the extent and type of direct criminal victimization experienced by the respondent or household members in the past 12 months, including frequency across specific crime types and whether the most recent incident was reported to authorities.

name: fear_and_risk_avoidance
description: Measures the behavioral and psychological impact of crime fear on daily life, including subjective fear of specific crimes, perceived probability of victimization, avoidance of places, and cessation of activities to reduce exposure to crime or violence.

name: punitive_and_vigilante_attitudes
description: Measures respondents' endorsement of coercive or extralegal responses to crime and insecurity, including support for capital punishment, community self-justice when authorities fail, the use of violence to combat violence, and preference for security over liberty.
